title: Day 2: 6-Client MCP Config Generation (Convention-over-Configuration)
description: Implement the ClientConfigWriter interface with 6 client-specific implementations to generate MCP server configuration files for Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Roo Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex CLI. Use convention-over-configuration: auto-detect client config locations by scanning workspace for .cursor/, .windsurf/, .roo/ etc., with fabric.config.json.clientPaths as explicit override. Claude Code requires dual write to both ~/.claude/settings.json (CLI) and ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json (Desktop). Codex uses @iarna/toml for ~/.codex/config.toml — not hand-rolled TOML. Include a JSON schema for fabric.config.json. Smoke-test docs confirm all 6 clients connect.

files.change: TypeScript interface with clientKind discriminant; write(serverPath: string, workspaceRoot: string): Promise<void> method; read/detect methods

files.target: JSON-based config writers (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Roo Code, Gemini CLI)
files.change: 5 implementations of ClientConfigWriter for JSON-format MCP config files; handles create-or-merge logic

files.target: TOML config writer for Codex CLI
files.change: CodexTOMLConfigWriter using @iarna/toml; reads ~/.codex/config.toml, merges mcp.servers entry, writes back
Scan workspace for .cursor/ .windsurf/ .roo/ dirs + ~/.claude/ + ~/.codex/ + GEMINI.md existence; return detected clients; fabric.config.json.clientPaths overrides
Detect both ~/.claude/settings.json (CLI) and ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json (Desktop); write mcpServers entry to both without interference
Schema defining clientPaths overrides, externalFixturePath, and other fabric.config.json options
Step-by-step verification: run fab config install; open each of 6 clients; invoke tools/list; record results
citty subcommand: fab config install [--clients <list>] — runs resolver + writes config for detected or specified clients

implementation: Define ClientConfigWriter interface in writer.ts with clientKind: ClaudeCodeCLI | ClaudeCodeDesktop | Cursor | Windsurf | RooCode | GeminiCLI | CodexCLI and write/detect methods

Implement JSON config writers for Cursor (.cursor/mcp.json), Windsurf (.windsurf/mcp.json), Roo Code (.roo/mcp.json), Gemini CLI (GEMINI.md or ~/.gemini/config.json per actual client spec), using create-or-merge JSON logic
Implement CodexTOMLConfigWriter in toml.ts using @iarna/toml; read ~/.codex/config.toml, parse, inject mcp.servers.fabric entry, write back; validate against Codex TOML schema

Implement Claude Code dual-write handler in claude-code.ts: detect both CLI settings path (~/.claude/settings.json) and Desktop app path (~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json); write mcpServers entry to each independently; if one path does not exist skip without error

Implement convention-over-config resolver in resolver.ts: scan cwd for .cursor/ .windsurf/ .roo/ existence; check ~/.claude/ and ~/.codex/ for CLI clients; return ClientKind[] of detected clients; allow fabric.config.json.clientPaths to override paths
Create fab config install command in commands/config.ts using citty; accept --clients flag for explicit selection; default to resolver output
Create schemas/fabric-config.json with $schema, clientPaths, externalFixturePath properties
Write unit tests covering all 6 client writers; write docs/day2-smoke-test.md with verification steps

reference.pattern: Convention-over-configuration client detection; JSON merge (not overwrite) for existing configs
reference.examples: mcp-first-fabric.md §2 for 6 target clients; synthesis.json decisions_locked.target_clients; @iarna/toml npm package for TOML parsing

criteria: ClientConfigWriter interface with exactly 6 clientKind implementations present in codebase; unit tests cover all 6 writers with pass rate 100%
CodexTOMLConfigWriter uses @iarna/toml (import verifiable in toml.ts); generated output matches ~/.codex/config.toml schema with valid mcp.servers section

Convention-over-config resolver auto-detects at least Cursor, Windsurf, Roo Code by scanning for .cursor/ .windsurf/ .roo/ directories in workspace root; fabric.config.json.clientPaths overrides respected

Claude Code dual write: both ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json receive mcpServers.fabric entry; running the command twice does not duplicate the entry
Real smoke test on dev machine: fab config install completes without error; all 6 clients show fabric-context-server in their tools/list invocation

rationale.chosen_approach: Discriminated union ClientConfigWriter interface with separate implementations per client; @iarna/toml for Codex as explicitly required by handoff spec
alternatives_considered: Generic template-based config generation (insufficient for client-specific edge cases like Claude dual-write)
Hand-rolled TOML serialization (explicitly forbidden by handoff spec — use @iarna/toml)
decision_factors: Handoff spec explicitly calls out @iarna/toml requirement for Codex
Claude Code dual-write is a unique requirement needing dedicated handler
Convention-over-config avoids requiring users to configure paths manually
rationale.tradeoffs: Separate per-client implementations add code volume but are necessary for correctness given each client has different config file formats and locations

manual_checks: Run fab config install in a workspace with .cursor/ .windsurf/ .roo/ directories — verify all 3 detected automatically
Inspect ~/.codex/config.toml after CodexTOMLConfigWriter runs — must be valid TOML with correct mcp.servers structure
Inspect ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json — both must have mcpServers.fabric entry
Open Cursor with updated .cursor/mcp.json — fab_get_rules must appear in tools list
Run fab config install twice — no duplicate entries in any config file
success_metrics: 6/6 client config writers produce valid output files
All 6 clients show 3 MCP tools in tools/list during smoke test

risks.description: Gemini CLI MCP config format may not be documented — actual config location/format unknown until Day 2 live test
risks.mitigation: Check gemini CLI --help and official docs before implementing GeminiCLI writer; treat as discoverable during implementation
risks.fallback: If Gemini CLI uses non-standard format, implement best-effort writer and document in smoke-test results

risks.description: Codex CLI MCP availability (Kill Switch 3): tools/list may fail
risks.mitigation: Day 2 is the detection point per brainstorm KS-3; if Codex MCP unavailable, document fallback (AGENTS.md direct read)
risks.fallback: Codex supports AGENTS.md natively — document fallback mode in smoke-test.md

interfaces.name: ClientConfigWriter
interfaces.definition: { clientKind: ClientKind; write(serverPath: string, workspaceRoot: string): Promise<void>; detect(workspaceRoot: string): Promise<boolean> }
interfaces.purpose: Unified interface for all 6 client MCP config writers

key_functions.purpose: Convention-over-config: scan workspace and return list of applicable writers
Read-modify-write JSON config file; add mcpServers.fabric without overwriting existing entries

classes.name: ClaudeCodeConfigWriter
classes.purpose: Dual-write to ~/.claude/settings.json and Desktop app config; implements ClientConfigWriter
